matches.moment: Morocco absorb Canada's late pressure and win it on the counter in the 82nd minute after the hosts commit numbers forward chasing an equalizer.
Paraguay's low block cracks in the 56th minute when a mistimed challenge in the box gifts France a penalty, breaking the deadlock and opening the floodgates.

verdict: Today's Round of 16 openers split cleanly along the line the data has drawn all tournament: one genuine coin-flip and one mismatch that the numbers refuse to soften. Canada's dream run - a maiden Round of 16 appearance built on a favorable Qatar mismatch and a stoppage-time winner - meets a Moroccan side that has quietly beaten Brazil and Netherlands to a draw and taken both prior meetings with the hosts; strip the padding from Canada's attacking numbers and the two sides are functionally even in quality, with Morocco's edge resting on defensive resilience against comparable opposition rather than raw firepower, which is why 2-1 rather than anything more emphatic is the call. Paraguay's story is different in kind, not degree - Orlando Gill has been the single best individual performer of their tournament, and his heroics against Germany prove this defense can hold for 120 minutes against real quality, but the underlying attacking data (xG in the 0.24-0.47 range across three matches) confirms this is a side without the tools to threaten a French front line that has scored at least three in every match it has played, Sweden included. Neither fixture carries the complacency or rotation risk that has produced this tournament's upsets to date - both favorites here have every reason to be at full strength - so today's slate is a legitimate test of quality rather than motivation, and the numbers say Morocco edges a coin-flip while France's ceiling proves too high for Paraguay's floor to hold. - The Analyst.

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.stat: 2.9 cumulative tournament xG; 8 goal involvements in 4 games
players.watch: Movement off Upamecano's side to isolate Paraguay's centre-backs 1v1

players.name: Orlando Gill
players.stat: 5 saves vs Germany (incl. 2 in the shootout), 6 shots on target saved vs Turkey, 5 vs Australia
players.watch: Reaction saves and 1-on-1s against Mbappé and Doué

players.name: Ismael Saibari
players.stat: 3 goals this tournament (team-high); scored the winning penalty vs Netherlands
players.watch: Late runs into the box from the right half-space

players.name: Jonathan David
players.stat: 3 goals this tournament (hat-trick vs Qatar); 42 goals in 80 caps, Canada's all-time top scorer
players.watch: Timing of runs in behind Diop/Riad

players.name: Achraf Hakimi
players.stat: Primary attacking outlet and set-piece delivery threat down the right
players.watch: Overlapping runs and delivery from the byline
